public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as clerk of arrivals.
appearance: Morra Ashvein carries the marks of clerk of arrivals in red dust and doorway ritual: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Keeps language exact, as if wording itself is part of the custody chain. In Psanan, that reads as someone heat-forged, direct, and honor-conscious, shaped by household authority and volcanic endurance, and the habits of a clerk of arrivals still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of clerk of arrivals in and around Ashwake Port, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Ashwake Port while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to honor loss is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Morra Ashvein needs help resolving a missing ledger, inconsistent testimony, or tampered record tied to Ashwake Port.
Morra Ashvein can entangle the party in seals, claims, or proof requirements that others are trying to blur on purpose.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Seals, ledgers, or carefully bundled papers make record authority the first thing Morra Ashvein communicates in public. In Ashwake Port, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Ash Port Factors, Great House Citadel Circle.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Ashwake Port: offices, toll points, ledgers, archives, or testimony desks
who_avoids_them: those hoping wording can stay vague

first_impression: Morra Ashvein presents as Keeps language exact, as if wording itself is part of the custody chain. In Psanan, that reads as someone heat-forged, direct, and honor-conscious, shaped by household authority and volcanic endurance, and the habits of a clerk of arrivals still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Clerk of Arrivals, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Psanan.
what_they_refuse: Morra Ashvein refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Clerk of Arrivals too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Morra Ashvein can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Morra Ashvein adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Morra Ashvein is Known locally through day-to-day work as clerk of arrivals. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Clerk of Arrivals.
social_texture: Morra Ashvein speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Ashwake Port, people read them as ceremony-conscious, prideful, and sharply aware of household honor.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to gifts and bridewealth; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but honor loss becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Morra touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Morra Ashvein when the party needs a socially grounded face for Ashwake Port: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
